other_observers: None.; I was the original finder and identifier of the bird. As I viewed the bird, I called a local contact, Scott Roederer, who called other local birders. Two arrived in time to see the bird farther east over the lake and on the water and to confirm the identification - Scott Rashid (who also took some photos of the bird) and Judie Wright. With Scott's permission I am submitting one of his photos of the bird. I did not have a camera with me. No one saw the bird after about 3:45 pm.
duration: 7 minutes sitting on the water and about 1 minute in flight (total of three flight observations)
specific_location: Lake Estes, seen in flight near the western end of the lake and then sitting on the water just below the water outflow from the power plant located at the west end of the lake.
description: As I walked the trail to the lake, I saw a gull flying away from me toward the lake. It had very distinct dark wing tips with a pattern of black and white in the wing in which the black areas formed a "v" shape on the front of each wing with a bright white area behind the black "v". I lost sight of it and attempted to relocate it. I saw it again in a few minutes flying away from me and found it sitting on the water about 100 feet from where I was observing. The gull had a white head, black eye, and black bill with a small black area immediately behind the eye and a wide black line/bar at the back of the head/neck separating the head from the back, as well as a lot of black in the folded wings, showing black in the wingtips and black on what appeared to be the median or lesser coverts. The bird had a gray mantle, and the nape of the neck was white behind the black line/bar. As the bird flew away the final time, I saw that it had a white tail with a black band at the tip.Behaviors: The bird was seen three times in flight briefly and seemed to have a typical gull flight. It sat on the water rather quietly for about 7 minutes before taking flight again.Call: NonePlumage: First winter
photographer: Photographer: photo by Scott Rashid submitted by James
